reasoning: The baseline price on 2025-04-25 is 113.99. The stock has rallied sharply by ~12% over the last 3 weeks from its early April lows of ~101, recovering right to the 114 level which had acted as a major support in December 2024 before breaking down. This previous support now likely acts as near-term technical resistance. Furthermore, this rally has occurred immediately prior to Q1 earnings (scheduled for April 28), which management pre-announced as weak ($0.45-$0.55 EPS) in mid-March. Because of the recent significant run-up into a weak earnings report, the stock is vulnerable to a "sell the news" pullback or failure to break resistance in the short term. Fundamentally, domestic steel producers face headwinds from paused/reduced federal stimulus spending (IRA/IIJA) despite potential tariff support. Thus, I assign a lower probability (0.44) for the near-term May 4 date, expecting a post-earnings rejection at the 114 resistance level. As the time horizon expands to May, July, and October, the general upward drift of equity markets and long-term value factors (evidenced by massive company buybacks at higher prices in Q1) begin to dominate, increasing uncertainty and pulling the probability steadily upwards toward baseline (0.50).
